title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description, eksperci KPMG omawiają, jak rozpoznawać restrukturyzacje z perspektywy cen transferowych i wycen oraz jakie obowiązki raportowe (TPR, MDR) i ryzyka się z nimi wiążą. Podkreślają znaczenie terminowej dokumentacji, roli wycen oraz typowych pułapek przy przenoszeniu produkcji, dystrybucji, pracowników i kontraktów, a na koniec proponują checklistę działań dla firm.
bullets: Definicja restrukturyzacji w kontekście cen transferowych i kryteria istotnej zmiany (analiza EBIT).
Znaczenie dokumentacji restrukturyzacji przygotowanej „na czas” dla ograniczenia ryzyka podatkowego.
Obowiązki raportowe: Transfer Pricing Report (TPR) i Mandatory Disclosure Rules (MDR).
Rola wycen, typowe błędy oraz klasyczny scenariusz przeniesienia produkcji lub dystrybucji.
Ryzyka przy transferze pracowników, przeniesieniu kontraktów i praktyczna checklista działań.
takeaways: Przed przeniesieniem aktywów lub działalności sprawdź wpływ na ceny transferowe i przygotuj wycenę — brak dokumentacji może zwiększyć ryzyko podatkowe.
Ustal, czy zmiana przekracza próg istotności (np. wpływ na EBIT) — to determinuje obowiązki raportowe (TPR/MDR).
Przy transferze zespołów lub kontraktów uwzględnij konsekwencje operacyjne i dokumentacyjne, aby uniknąć późniejszych sporów.
Wdrożenie checklisty restrukturyzacyjnej i terminowa dokumentacja upraszczają due diligence i audyt podatkowy.
Zadbaj o rzetelne wyceny i ocenę typowych pułapek, by minimalizować korekty podatkowe i nieporozumienia regulatorów.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Marcus Oliver, lead of Google Delta's professional services team, says the most expensive AI mistake is rushing to code without a strategy rather than overspending on compute. He advises escaping the "coding trap" by reimagining value chains and deploying multi-agent systems to deliver real business outcomes.
bullets: The costliest AI error is coding first without a strategic plan, not compute expenses.
Avoid the "coding trap" by defining clear business value before building.
Reimagine value chains to identify where AI creates tangible impact.
Deploy multi-agent systems as a way to drive practical, outcome-focused solutions.
Leverage professional services expertise (Google Delta) to shape strategy and execution.
takeaways: Prioritize strategy and business impact over immediate coding or raw compute purchases.
Map AI projects to specific value-chain outcomes before committing infrastructure.
Consider multi-agent architectures for automating and optimizing data-center or grid operations.
Align procurement and capacity planning with measurable business outcomes to avoid costly rework.
Engage professional services to design a strategic implementation roadmap and reduce execution risk.

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this replay episode reintroduces the kickoff of PwC’s Reporting reset series focused on presentation fundamentals for year‑end reporting. It covers foundational GAAP and SEC presentation requirements, reporting period decisions, balance sheet and income statement presentation, accounting changes and error corrections, and subsequent events, with links to related PwC guidance.
bullets: Overview of foundational GAAP and SEC requirements for financial statement presentation
Guidance on selecting appropriate reporting periods
Balance sheet presentation: classification, required disclosures, and best practices
Income statement presentation: structure and key considerations
Treatment and disclosure of accounting changes, estimates, error corrections, and subsequent events
takeaways: Ensure financial statements meet GAAP and SEC presentation requirements to support clear external reporting and compliance.
Confirm chosen reporting periods accurately reflect your operations and any significant timing differences.
Validate balance sheet and income statement classifications and disclosures to provide transparent financial position and performance.
Track and document accounting changes, estimates, and error corrections so they are applied and disclosed consistently.
Identify and disclose subsequent events promptly to reflect material developments occurring after the reporting date.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description, DeepKeep on Google Cloud is a unified platform designed to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions by providing total visibility to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats. It offers AI Lens for visibility and policy enforcement, AI Red Teaming for stress-testing models and applications, and an AI Firewall to apply real-time guardrails to prompts and responses.
bullets: Unified platform for securing AI agents, applications, and user interactions.
AI Lens provides visibility and policy enforcement across AI interactions.
AI Red Teaming enables stress-testing against real-world attacks.
AI Firewall secures prompts and responses in real time with built-in guardrails.
Aimed at eliminating shadow AI and protecting infrastructure from evolving threats.
takeaways: Visibility and policy controls help reduce unmonitored AI usage (shadow AI) across the organization.
Built-in red teaming can identify vulnerabilities before adversaries exploit them.
Real-time firewalling of prompts/responses adds an operational layer of defense for deployed AI systems.
A unified solution can simplify security management for AI agents and applications at scale.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this GeForce Garage video celebrates the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc community build contest, u/JeffersonPutnam, who assembled a small-form-factor PC featuring a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition. The description lists the full parts and links, and notes GeForce host Dwight (_MightyPotato).
bullets: Contest winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — built an SFF GeForce RTX 5090 rig
Main components linked on PCPartPicker (full spec available)
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition
Other key parts: MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS
GeForce host Dwight (_MightyPotato) and official NVIDIA GeForce social links included
takeaways: The build demonstrates RTX 5090 deployment in a small-form-factor desktop, paired with a 240mm AIO and a 1000W SFX PSU — indicating a notable power and cooling budget for compact systems.
A complete PCPartPicker parts list is provided, useful for compatibility checks and replication of the configuration.
The rig pairs a high-end Ryzen 7 9800X3D and DDR5-6000 RAM with the RTX 5090, showing typical modern CPU/memory choices alongside flagship GPUs.
This presentation is community-driven (r/buildapc contest), highlighting direct consumer engagement around GeForce RTX 50-series hardware.
